title: Microsoft Graph - Entra ID Users, Groups, And Service Principal Management
applies_to: Automating user and group management via Microsoft Graph PowerShell or REST
Service principal and app registration lifecycle management
Querying Entra ID RBAC role assignments programmatically
Granting Microsoft Graph application permissions to service principals

confirmed_facts.fact: List users: `GET /users?$select=id,displayName,userPrincipalName,accountEnabled,assignedLicenses&$top=999`. Use `$select` to limit fields returned — improves performance and reduces token size. Paginate via `@odata.nextLink`. Filter disabled accounts: `$filter=accountEnabled eq false`.

List group members: `GET /groups/{groupId}/members?$select=id,displayName,userPrincipalName`. Returns a collection of directoryObject. To get only users (exclude nested groups, devices): `GET /groups/{groupId}/members/microsoft.graph.user`. For transitive membership: `GET /groups/{groupId}/transitiveMembers`.

List service principals (app registrations): `GET /servicePrincipals?$select=id,appId,displayName,appRoles,oauth2PermissionScopes`. Filter by app ID: `$filter=appId eq '{appId}'`. This returns the service principal object including the list of app roles the SP exposes.

Grant an application permission (app role) to a service principal (app-only access): `POST /servicePrincipals/{spObjectId}/appRoleAssignments` with body `{ "principalId": "<SP object ID>", "resourceId": "<Graph SP object ID>", "appRoleId": "<role ID from Graph SP appRoles>" }`. Requires AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All permission. This is how Managed Identity permissions are assigned programmatically.

Look up the Graph service principal object ID (needed for appRoleAssignments): `GET /servicePrincipals?$filter=appId eq '00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000'`. The Graph API's fixed appId is always `00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000`. The `id` property of the returned object is the resourceId for appRoleAssignment calls.

Look up specific app role IDs for permissions like Sites.ReadWrite.All: `GET /servicePrincipals?$filter=appId eq '00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000'&$select=appRoles`. Iterate the `appRoles` array and find the entry where `value` matches the permission name (e.g., 'Sites.ReadWrite.All'). Its `id` is the `appRoleId` for the assignment.

List Entra ID directory role assignments: `GET /roleManagement/directory/roleAssignments?$filter=principalId eq '{userId}'&$expand=roleDefinition`. Returns the Entra ID roles (e.g., Global Reader, Security Reader) assigned to a user or service principal. Requires RoleManagement.Read.Directory permission.

Check group membership for a user or service principal: `POST /directoryObjects/{id}/getMemberObjects` with body `{ "securityEnabledOnly": false }`. Returns all group and role IDs the object is a direct or transitive member of. Faster than iterating all groups when checking permissions.

implementation_notes: When granting Graph permissions to a Managed Identity via PowerShell, combine the five steps: get MI service principal ID, get Graph SP object ID, get the app role ID for the target permission, POST appRoleAssignment. Wrap in a function `Grant-GraphPermission -PrincipalId $miObjectId -PermissionName 'Sites.ReadWrite.All'`.
Use $select on all list queries. Without $select, the API returns all default properties — for users this includes many fields that are rarely needed and inflate response size.

For audit reporting: `GET /auditLogs/directoryAudits?$filter=activityDateTime ge {dateTime}&$select=activityDateTime,activityDisplayName,initiatedBy,targetResources` returns Entra ID admin audit events. Requires AuditLog.Read.All permission.
caveats: appRoleAssignment grants are tenant-wide and take effect immediately. Incorrect grants (wrong appRoleId or too-broad permissions) are a security risk. Always verify the permission name from the appRoles array before assigning.
Group member listing returns a maximum of 100 members per page by default. Large groups (>1000 members) require pagination via @odata.nextLink.
Directory role assignment queries require the caller to have RoleManagement.Read.Directory or higher. This permission requires admin consent and is not granted by default.
